10 January 2026

DYCP info session

ABOUT PLAY OFFICE

2025

Play Office  aims to explore local heritage as inspiration for placemaking and artistic works. Play Office is a incubation space for creatives to develop dynamic, future focused, responsive practices.

Artist Development

Education and mentorship opportunities to artists who want to engage with Heritage and New Technologies. Funded time to work on new ideas and projects. Encouraging collaboration and skill-sharing internationally.  

Equipment and Space

A free library of equipment needed for working in XR and immersive technologies. Free studio space for emerging artists and creatives in Portsmouth. 

Authentic Experiences

Curate and produce artworks and experiences that foster a sense of empathy and community leading to greater cohesion and social wellbeing

2024

ABOUT PLAY OFFICE

ARTIST DEVELOPMNT - Resonate (ii) Artist Cohort

2025

Play Office hosts Resonate - an artist development programme supporting emerging artists to engage with technology in their practice and gain both creative and professional support in developing their careers in the arts.

Harry Payne

APPLY NOW

RESONATE (ii)

For it's 2nd year Resonate artist development opportunity returns to Portsmouth, working with artists to develop skills in new technology and immersive practices with a focus on local heritage.

OPPORTUNITIES

2024

BLOG

2025

Resonate (ii) Touring Open Studio - XR and Heritage

In 2025, Play Office travelled to Cairo to present Resonate II, Open Studio at Medrar for Contemporary Art, sharing a live snapshot of a Portsmouth-based programme in motion. This was not a finished exhibition shipped from one context to another, it was an open studio, a working process, and an invitation, to test how a project shifts when it meets a new city, a new audience, and new questions. Medrar’s programme framed Resonate II through a powerful provocation, can interpre

AlterCare: A Worldbuilding Workshop with Beirut Art Centre

What happens when you treat an ecology, not as a backdrop, but as the engine of a world? At Beirut Art Center, Play Office ran an XR workshop with artist and Resonate Alumni Lara Kobeissi, inviting participants to explore ecologies, worldbuilding, and emerging technologies as one connected creative practice. The goal was not to learn software in isolation, it was to build a shared pipeline where lived environments, material traces, and embodied movement could become the found
